pr.title: docs: fix stale roadmap, Docker tags, and status contradictions
pr.body: ## Summary Fix stale documentation across roadmap, status, README, and changelog that contradicts the actual project state. All changes are documentation-only accuracy corrections. ## Why A docs accuracy audit found several contradictions between documentation files and the actual repo/release state: 1. **Docker examples used `v1.0.0` tag** — current releases are v1.2.x (latest stable: v1.2.6) 2. **ROADMAP claimed MSI Installer as simply "Released"** — MSI was v1.0.0 era; portable ZIP is the canonical method since v1.1.3+ 3. **ROADMAP claimed "Versioned JSON & CSV export" as "In Progress"** — CSV has zero implementation (no code exists); JSON output is released but versioned schema is not yet implemented 4. **STATUS.md said security hardening was "In Progress"** — contradicts ROADMAP.md which correctly says "Released v1.2.x"; CI confirms cargo-deny, cargo-audit, and fuzz smoke are all active 5. **CHANGELOG v1.1.3 referenced "v2.0 capabilities"** — project is at v1.x; should reference v1.1.3 ## Changes - [x] Documentation updated Files changed: - `README.md` — update Docker pull examples from `v1.0.0` → `v1.2.6` - `docs/ROADMAP.md` — clarify MSI as superseded; correct CSV export status from In Progress to Planned - `docs/STATUS.md` — align security hardening status with ROADMAP (In Progress → Released v1.2.x) - `CHANGELOG.md` — fix stale "v2.0" version reference to "v1.1.3" ## Testing ```bash cargo check --workspace # PASS (exit 0) cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings # PASS (exit 0) ``` No code changes — documentation-only fixes. ## Compatibility / Risk Notes - CLI compatibility impact: None - Output/report format impact: None - Operational risk and rollback plan: Zero risk, documentation-only ## Security Considerations None — documentation-only changes. ## Checklist - [x] I followed the contribution guidelines in `CONTRIBUTING.md` - [x] I ran relevant checks locally - [x] I updated docs/changelog as needed - [x] I confirmed no sensitive data is included Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/871fb66468ba497e98d69036cb2a90fc Requested by: @benjisho ---
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
